channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: New Rogue One Prequel Comics to Explore Characters from the Film
item.description: Marvel Comics, in collaboration with Lucasfilm, is set to release a series of prequel comics based on the characters from 'Rogue One: A Star Wars Story.' These new comics follow the success of the 'An...

item.title: Exceptional Wildlife Photography from Around the World
item.description: Every year, wildlife photographers capture rare and extraordinary moments in nature, revealing the beauty and fragility of the natural world. This curated collection showcases some of the most remarka...

item.title: Dutch Prime Minister-designate Rob Jetten reaffirms past criticisms of Donald Trump ahead of inauguration
item.description: Rob Jetten, leader of the Dutch liberal party D66 and the incoming Prime Minister of the Netherlands set to be sworn in shortly after February 21, 2026, has confirmed he stands by his previous strong ...

item.title: Matty Healy and Gabbriette Bechtel reportedly plan July wedding in California
item.description: According to a report by The Sun, Matty Healy, lead singer of The 1975, is reportedly set to marry model and singer Gabbriette Bechtel in July 2026 in California. This potential wedding would occur ju...

item.title: Liquid Death and Spotify Launch Limited-Edition Urn-Shaped Bluetooth Speaker with Personalized Playlist Feature
item.description: Liquid Death has partnered with Spotify to release a limited-edition product called the Eternal Playlist Urn, an urn-shaped container that doubles as a Bluetooth speaker. Staying true to Liquid Death’...

item.title: Pennsylvania Box Huckleberry Colony Estimated to Be Up to 13,000 Years Old
item.description: A colony of box huckleberry shrubs in rural Pennsylvania is considered one of the oldest living organisms on Earth, with scientists estimating its age at up to 13,000 years. The colony, known as Losh ...

item.title: CPJ reports unprecedented number of journalist deaths in 2025, with majority attributed to Israeli military actions
item.description: The Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) reported that 2025 was the deadliest year for journalists since it began collecting data more than three decades ago, with a record 129 press members killed ...

item.title: Alberto Seveso Explores the Art of Ink Through Underwater Color Photography
item.description: The article explores the artistic power and enduring legacy of ink as a medium, focusing on the underwater color photography of Alberto Seveso. For thousands of years, ink has been used to depict the ...

item.title: The Benefits of Buying Windows and Doors from Authorized Dealers
item.description: The article explains why selecting an authorized dealer for windows and doors is a critical decision for homeowners seeking quality, durability, and long-term value. While many buyers focus on style, ...

item.title: Essential Maintenance Tips to Preserve Your Property’s Appearance
item.description: Maintaining the visual appeal of a property requires proactive care and understanding the factors that cause damage over time. Environmental elements like pollutants, mineral deposits, algae, and UV r...

item.title: Lily Allen Announces Relationship with Jonah Freud, Descendant of Sigmund Freud
item.description: Lily Allen has officially confirmed her new relationship with Jonah Freud, the great-great-grandson of the renowned psychoanalyst Sigmund Freud. This announcement comes nearly a year after her separat...

item.title: Chinese Researchers Test Cyanobacteria-Based Soil Stabilization to Combat Desertification
item.description: Chinese researchers are testing an experimental biotechnology designed to accelerate the stabilization of desert soils using cyanobacteria. The project is being carried out at the Shapotou research st...

item.title: Russia Increases Oil Exports Despite Falling Revenues
item.description: Despite ongoing Western sanctions and efforts to block its oil trade since the beginning of the Ukraine War four years ago, Russia's crude oil exports have not diminished. According to a report by the...

item.title: Remarkable Trees Across the Globe and Their Unique Features
item.description: Trees are some of the most extraordinary living organisms on Earth, offering not only beauty but also a glimpse into history and survival. This article highlights several remarkable trees from around ...

item.title: Average U.S. Families Struggle to Afford Childcare, Study Shows
item.description: A recent LendingTree study highlights the growing unaffordability of childcare in the United States. Federal guidelines consider childcare affordable if it consumes no more than 7% of household income...

item.title: Omar Yaghi develops solar-powered device to harvest 1,000 liters of water from air daily
item.description: Professor Omar Yaghi, a chemist at the University of California, Berkeley and 2025 Nobel Prize winner, has developed an innovative device capable of producing up to 1,000 liters of clean water daily f...

item.title: Anonymous 15-Year-Old Girl Describes Daily Exposure to Misogynistic Abuse and Objectification on Social Media
item.description: In this anonymous opinion piece, a 15-year-old British schoolgirl details the pervasive and inescapable misogyny she encounters daily on mainstream platforms like Instagram and TikTok. Despite activel...

item.title: War Losses, Falling Birthrates and Migration Deepen Ukraine’s Demographic Crisis
item.description: As the war with Russia approaches its fourth year, Ukraine is facing a severe demographic crisis marked by heavy military casualties, plummeting birth rates, and mass migration. According to leading d...

item.title: US Women’s Olympic Hockey Team Declines State of the Union Invitation Due to Schedule Conflicts
item.description: The gold medal-winning U.S. women’s Olympic hockey team has declined an invitation to attend President Donald Trump’s State of the Union address, citing prior scheduling conflicts with academic and pr...

item.title: How Age-Verification Laws Are Expanding Digital Surveillance and Undermining Privacy
item.description: The article examines the growing push for mandatory age-verification systems on social media platforms and argues that these measures create a structural conflict with data-privacy principles. As lawm...
